concerns.text: T014 (benchmark accuracy) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on T012 (classification logic). The task description says 'Depends on: T012', yet the [P] tag implies it can run in parallel with T012. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule; the classifier must exist before it can be benchmarked.

T018 and T018.5 (reliability metrics) are marked [P] and depend on T012. However, T027 (sensitivity analysis) depends on T018/T018.5. If T018/T018.5 are truly parallel to T012, T027 cannot start until they finish. The current grouping suggests T018/18.5 are independent of T019 (ANCOVA), but T027 depends on T019 AND T018. The dependency chain T012 -> T018 -> T027 is valid, but the [P] tag on T018/18.5 is misleading if they are blocking T027 which also depends on T019. More critically, T027 depends on T019 (ANCOVA) which depends on T012. T018 also depends on T012. T027 depends on T018. This creates a valid chain, but the [P] tag on T018/18.5 suggests they can run in parallel with T019, which is true, but the text 'Depends on: T012' is correct. The issue is T014's [P] tag vs its explicit dependency on T012.

T024 (regression test) depends on T019 and T019.5. T019.5 (reference script) depends on T012. T019 depends on T012. T024 is marked [P] but depends on T019 (ANCOVA) and T019.5. Since T019 is the main analysis, T024 cannot run in parallel with T019. The [P] tag is incorrect here as it implies independence from T019.

T036 (low balance alert) depends on T012. It is marked [P]. However, T036 requires reading `data/derived/classified_data.csv` which is the output of T012. If T012 is not complete, T036 cannot run. The [P] tag is acceptable if T012 is considered a 'foundational' task that is already done, but in the context of the user story block, T036 is listed under 'Phase 6: Polish' and depends on T012. If T012 is part of US1, and T036 is in Phase 6, the dependency is valid. The [P] tag is problematic if it implies T036 can run before T012 completes. The text says 'Depends on: T012', so the [P] tag is likely a mistake if it implies parallelism with T012.
T037 (VIF visualization) depends on T019 (ANCOVA). It is marked [P]. T019 is the main analysis task. T037 cannot run in parallel with T019 as it consumes T019's output. The [P] tag is incorrect.
T038 (power analysis) depends on T019 (ANCOVA). It is marked [P]. Similar to T037, T038 consumes T019's output and cannot run in parallel with T019. The [P] tag is incorrect.

The 'Checkpoint' after Phase 3 states 'User Story 1 should be fully functional'. However, T014 (benchmark) and T018/18.5 (reliability) are listed in Phase 3 but depend on T012. If T012 is the last task in US1, then T014/18/18.5 can run after T012. The [P] tag on T014/18/18.5 is acceptable if they run in parallel with each other after T012, but the text 'Depends on: T012' is clear. The issue is the [P] tag on T014 which explicitly depends on T012, making it not parallel to T012.
